text: The incidence of lower extremity running injuries in long distance runners ranges from 19.4% to 79.3%.
The knee is the predominant site of lower extremity running injuries in long distance runners.
A long training distance per week is a risk factor for running injuries in male runners.
A history of previous injuries is a risk factor for future lower extremity running injuries.
A gradual increase in training distance per week is a protective factor against knee injuries in runners.
